title: gaultheria × jordanensis
intro: Gaultheria × jordanensis, a hybrid plant resulting from the cross between Gaultheria procumbens and Gaultheria shallon, is a type of evergreen shrub native to the western coast of North America. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its rich content of salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which possesses anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. Gaultheria × jordanensis has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its expectorant and antiseptic properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Gaultheria × jordanensis are typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate colds, coughs, and sore throats.
